content:
I am delighted to announce that our research chapter has been published in the recently released book Indian Knowledge Systems (2026).
The book is a significant scholarly initiative that brings together diverse perspectives on Indian Knowledge Systems, highlighting how Bhāratīya knowledge traditions can contribute to addressing contemporary societal, legal, managerial, and environmental challenges. Inspired by the Capacity Building Programmes organized by Rajiv Gandhi National University of Law Punjab (RGNUL)and Law Teachers India the volume aims to bridge traditional wisdom with modern knowledge systems.
About Our Chapter
Chapter Number :1 
Chapter Title: Bridging Faith and Science: Ecological Awareness in Jain and Vedic Traditions
In this chapter, my co-author Aarohi Dave and I explore how the ecological wisdom embedded in Jain and Vedic traditions aligns with contemporary environmental science. By revisiting principles such as Ahimsa, Aparigraha, and Ṛta, we demonstrate how ancient Indian knowledge offers valuable insights for sustainability, environmental ethics, and responsible living in today's world.
I sincerely thank the editors Prof. (Dr.) Jai Shankar Singh, Dr. Sangeeta Taak, and Dr. Kalpeshkumar L Gupta for their vision in bringing together this important volume. I am also grateful to my co-author, Aarohi Dave for her wonderful collaboration.
A special thanks to Karnavati University (KU) and the Unitedworld Institute of Design (UID) for their constant encouragement, valuable inputs, and unwavering support throughout this research journey.
Looking forward to continuing meaningful conversations on the relevance of Indian Knowledge Systems in contemporary research and practice.
https://amzn.in/d/05tUdvEu

comment: Interdisciplinary work here hinges on how you operationalize concepts like ahimsa or ṛta into measurable environmental indicators without flattening their philosophical context or forcing direct equivalence with modern scientific terms.
